bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. All issues have the same value ordering (valueC > valueB > valueA), so my optimal bid is valueC for every issue. This gives me utility of 1.0 across all issues, weighted appropriately.

We're very early (t=0.02). I opened with my ideal bid (all valueC = utility 1.0), and the opponent countered with their ideal bid (all valueA = utility 0.2 for me). The emotional message is cheap talk - their bid is completely opposite to mine. I should stay close to my top utility but make a small concession to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll concede on issueA and issueB (lowest importance, 0.1 each) by moving to valueB, while holding firm on the more important issues.

We're very early (t=0.04). The opponent is sticking rigidly to all-valueA offers (worst for me) despite emotional appeals - their behavior contradicts their words about "fair partnership." I should hold firm on my high-importance issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, issueD) while showing slight flexibility on low-importance ones. I'll keep valueC on issueF (weight 0.3) and the other high-weight issues, and concede a bit more on issueA and issueB (weight 0.1 each) by moving to valueB or even valueA.

The opponent is repeatedly offering all valueA across the board, which gives me minimal utility (0.20 on everything). Their emotional messages are contradicted by their bidding behavior - they're holding firm at the worst possible offer for me. We're very early (t=0.06), so I don't need to make large concessions yet. I'll continue holding firm on my high-importance issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, issueD) at valueC, while making a small additional concession on the low-importance issues (issueA, issueB) to signal willingness to negotiate.

The opponent has been consistently bidding all valueA across every issue, which is their best case and my worst case. Their emotional messages are cheap talk - the numbers show they haven't moved at all. We're still very early (t=0.08), so I have plenty of time. I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, issueD) while I've already conceded issueA and issueB to valueA. My last bid was {A:valueA, B:valueA, C:valueC, D:valueC, E:valueC, F:valueC} with utility = 0.1*0.2 + 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.04+0.04+0.2+0.2+0.2+0.3 = 0.98. I'll keep this bid since the opponent shows zero movement and time is still early.

The opponent is stubbornly bidding all valueA on every issue (their latest offer gives me utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.2*0.61 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.2 = very low). Their emotional messages are cheap talk - the numbers tell the real story. They made a tiny concession on issueD (valueB) in their last offer. I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, issueD) while giving on the low-weight ones (issueA, issueB). My current bid already concedes issueA and issueB to valueA. I'll maintain my strong position since it's still early (t=0.10), keeping all the important issues at valueC.

The opponent has been consistently offering all valueA across the board, with only a slight concession on issueD (moving to valueB at t=0.10 and t=0.12). We're still very early (t=0.12), so I should hold firm on my high-importance issues (issueF at 0.3, issueC/D/E at 0.2) while showing slight flexibility on lower-importance issues (issueA, issueB at 0.1). I'll maintain my current strong position on the high-weight issues while keeping issueA and issueB at valueA to signal some willingness to concede on less important items.

The opponent is consistently bidding all-valueA (or nearly so), which gives me very low utility. They're not conceding meaningfully - only issueD moved to valueB once. Their emotional messages are cheap talk contradicted by their stubborn bidding. We're still early (t=0.14), so I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, issueD) while giving up on low-weight issues (issueA, issueB) which I've already conceded. I'll maintain my current strong position since there's no real pressure yet.
